name: API Key Management (canonical)
description: Self-service creation, listing, rotation, revocation, and per-key usage stats for platform API keys (cdb_ prefix), with per-tier key-count limits and per-tier daily/monthly request quotas. The one implementation of API-key CRUD that is auth-gated, ownership-scoped, tier-limit-correct, and has real regression coverage — reused correctly by several other flows (trial-key signup, Google/SSO login auto-provisioning, marketplace checkout) rather than reimplemented by them.

frontend.evidence: frontend/user-dashboard.html:2129-2199 loadKeys()/createKey() call apiFetch('/api/keys'); frontend/index.html has two independent embedded widgets calling the same route (:16656-16699 and :18641-18743, cdbGenerateAPIKey/cdbRevokeAPIKey); frontend/customer-success-dashboard.html:585 is a read-only apiFetch('/api/keys') display.

navigation.evidence: frontend/user-dashboard.html nav includes a '🔑 API Keys' item (also visible in the live production dashboard) leading to a dedicated API Key Management section on that page.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/keyUsageBola.test.mjs:11 imports handleKeyUsage directly (tests a real BOLA/IDOR fix — workers/src/handlers/apikeys.js:133-139's ownership check). workers/test/phase4PremiumFeatures.test.mjs:18 imports handleRotateKey. workers/test/userPlanKeyLimit.test.mjs:9 and workers/test/phase8EntitlementTruth.test.mjs:20 import TIER_LIMITS from workers/src/auth/apiKeys.js. handleListKeys, handleCreateKey, and handleRevokeKey (workers/src/handlers/apikeys.js) have no direct import-based test coverage found (grep across workers/test/ for these three names returns 0 matches) — the create/list/revoke HTTP handlers themselves are the one real coverage gap in an otherwise well-tested system.

verification.evidence: workers/src/auth/apiKeys.js and workers/src/handlers/apikeys.js read in full. Router gates independently confirmed: /api/keys (GET/POST) gates on isRealUser(authCtx) at workers/src/index.js:2064 before dispatch; the three /api/keys/:id/* sub-routes (usage, rotate, DELETE) have no router-level isRealUser call but each handler independently enforces `if (!authCtx.user_id) return 401` plus ownership-scoped queries (workers/src/handlers/apikeys.js:21,76,116,129,138) — functionally equivalent protection via a different, less consistent mechanism, not a gap. Confirmed handleCreateKey enforces MAX_KEYS_BY_TIER (workers/src/handlers/apikeys.js:17) matching the pricing page's advertised per-tier key counts (comment at :12-16 documents a prior MSSP-tier miss, already fixed). Confirmed three independent, correctly-implemented consumers of the canonical createApiKey(db,userId,tier,label) 4-arg signature in the right order: workers/src/handlers/developerOnboardingHandler.js:180 (public trial-key signup funnel), workers/src/handlers/auth.js:96, workers/src/handlers/googleAuth.js:158, workers/src/handlers/ssoAuth.js:170, workers/src/handlers/marketplaceCheckoutHandler.js:610 — none of these reimplement key creation; all correctly call the canonical function. Full backend suite green (see docs/capability-registry/PROGRAM_BOARD.md session log for this wave's run).

notes: This is the healthy baseline against which the other three entries in this domain are contrasted. A prior (uncommitted, lost) session's investigation described '4-5 parallel API-key systems' in this codebase; this wave independently re-verified that claim and found it accurate — in fact 5 independent create/list/revoke implementations exist (this one, plus CAP-DEVPORTAL-002, -003, -004 below, plus a 5th scoped to MSSP-partner-issued customer keys in workers/src/handlers/msspTenantPlatform.js against its own separate mssp_customer_api_keys table — that one is out of scope for this domain and belongs to the already-populated mssp.json domain; it has real ownership/tier gating and test coverage via workers/test/deadAdminChecksRestored.test.mjs, not flagged here as a problem). Three of the five hashing implementations (workers/src/auth/apiKeys.js, workers/src/handlers/developerPortal.js, workers/src/handlers/msspTenantPlatform.js) independently reimplement the identical SHA-256-hex hashing logic rather than sharing one function — a duplication smell, not a bug by itself, but the root cause of why the two genuinely broken systems below (CAP-DEVPORTAL-003, -004) drifted out of sync with the real api_keys schema: each system owns its own INSERT statement instead of going through the one function (workers/src/auth/apiKeys.js's createApiKey()) that is actually kept in sync with schema migrations.

name: Self-Service Automation API Keys
description: A second, independent API-key create/list/revoke/rotate implementation exposed through the Automation dashboard, intended to let self-service (including ENTERPRISE-tier) customers generate keys for automation/workflow integrations.

frontend.evidence: frontend/automation-dashboard.html:489 loadKeys() → apiFetch('/api/self/keys'); :510 createKey() → apiPost('/api/self/keys',{label}) (hits the confirmed tier-mis-assignment bug below on every call); :526 revokeKey(); :533 rotateKey() calls POST /api/self/keys/{id}/rotate, which has no matching handler anywhere in workers/src/handlers/enterpriseAutomation.js's dispatch table — confirmed by grep, this button 404s on every click, not just intermittently.

navigation.evidence: Not verified this pass whether frontend/automation-dashboard.html itself is linked from primary navigation — the domain wave focused on the API-key logic reachable from it, not the page's own discoverability. Flagging as unknown rather than guessing.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/enterpriseAutomationTeamManagement.test.mjs imports handleAutoRoute from the same file but only exercises /api/auto/team — grep for handleCreateSelfKey/handleListSelfKeys/handleRevokeSelfKey across workers/test/ returns 0 matches. The confirmed tier-mis-assignment bug below has never been caught by CI.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/enterpriseAutomation.js:120-154 read directly. CONFIRMED bug — parameter-ordering mismatch at :127: `createApiKey(D, userId(authCtx), orgId(authCtx), userTier(authCtx), label)` calls the canonical 4-parameter `createApiKey(db, userId, userTier, label)` (workers/src/auth/apiKeys.js:66) with 5 positional arguments. Actual binding: userTier parameter receives orgId(authCtx) (an org-id string, never a valid TIER_LIMITS key), and the real tier string ends up bound to the label parameter instead — the caller's real intended label (the 5th argument) is silently discarded since the function only declares 4 parameters. Inside createApiKey (workers/src/auth/apiKeys.js:70): `const limits = TIER_LIMITS[userTier] || TIER_LIMITS.FREE;` — since userTier here is actually an org id, this line always falls through to TIER_LIMITS.FREE regardless of the caller's real plan, and the row's own `tier` column (workers/src/auth/apiKeys.js:79-81, bound positionally from the same corrupted variable) stores the org id instead of the account tier. Net effect, reproduced by tracing the exact call: an ENTERPRISE customer creating a self-service key via this route silently receives a FREE-tier-limited key with a garbage label, with no error surfaced anywhere. A second, related bug in the same file: handleListSelfKeys (:139) calls `getKeyUsageSummary(D, userId(authCtx))` — the real signature is `getKeyUsageSummary(db, keyId, userId)` (workers/src/auth/apiKeys.js:159) — so it passes the caller's own userId as keyId and leaves the real userId argument undefined; effect not fully traced this pass (getKeyUsageSummary's behavior on an unrecognized keyId + undefined userId), flagged for the dedicated fix to also check. Confirmed the rotate route (frontend/automation-dashboard.html:533) has no backend handler — grep of workers/src/handlers/enterpriseAutomation.js's handleAutoRoute dispatch table for a 'rotate' case matching /api/self/keys/*/rotate returns 0 matches.

notes: CONFIRMED real, live production bug — re-verified independently against current code (not carried over from the prior lost session's narration, per this registry's own evidence rule). Deliberately NOT fixed in this wave: this is a registry-population pass (discover, map, verify, register), not a fix sprint — matches the established pattern from the MASOC domain (CAP-MASOC-001), whose auth-gate finding was documented here first and fixed in a separate, dedicated, CAB-reviewed change afterward. Recommend the same treatment here: a bounded fix sprint scoped to (1) correcting the createApiKey call-site argument order in workers/src/handlers/enterpriseAutomation.js:127, (2) correcting the getKeyUsageSummary call-site argument order at the same file's line 139, (3) either implementing the missing rotate route or removing the dead button from frontend/automation-dashboard.html, (4) adding regression tests (currently zero) asserting a created key's stored tier matches authCtx.tier, not orgId. Business impact: every ENTERPRISE (and STARTER/PRO) customer who has used this specific self-service flow got a FREE-tier-limited key without being told — a real, silent customer-impacting defect, not just a code-quality issue.

name: Developer Portal — API Explorer, SDK Generation, OpenAPI/Postman Docs, and Key Self-Serve
description: The platform's self-documenting developer portal: a machine-generated API catalog (API_CATALOG), OpenAPI 3.1 spec, Postman collection, multi-language SDK download/generation, a request playground, and its own independent API-key create/list/revoke/rotate endpoints — documented as the very first step ('Get your API key') in this portal's own published quickstart guide.

frontend.evidence: frontend/automation-dashboard.html:890-1005 calls this portal's documentation/SDK/OpenAPI/Postman endpoints (sdk/download, quickstart, the OpenAPI spec route, the Postman collection route, auth-guide, migration-guide, version-policy, examples) — those endpoints were not individually re-verified against the live schema this pass (out of the two specific leads this wave was scoped to re-check) and are presumed functional pending a dedicated audit. No frontend page anywhere in frontend/*.html calls /api/developer/keys, /api/developer/keys/{id}, or /api/developer/keys/{id}/rotate (confirmed by grep) — the broken key endpoints below are reachable only by a developer calling the documented API directly, exactly as this portal's own quickstart instructs them to.

navigation.evidence: No dedicated frontend page for this portal exists; reachable only via direct API calls per its own self-published OpenAPI spec and quickstart guide (workers/src/handlers/developerPortal.js:1102), not via any customer-facing UI navigation path.

test_coverage.evidence: grep for workers/src/handlers/developerPortal.js / '/api/developer/keys' across workers/test/ returns 0 matches — zero test coverage of any kind for this file's key endpoints. Not independently checked this pass whether the non-key endpoints (SDK generation, OpenAPI spec, etc.) have coverage elsewhere.

verification.evidence: workers/src/handlers/developerPortal.js:971-1021 read directly. CONFIRMED bug — createAPIKey (:978-981) issues `INSERT INTO api_keys (id,org_id,name,key_hash,scopes,expires_at,status,created_at,updated_at) VALUES (...)`. Independently cross-checked against the live schema (workers/schema_bootstrap.sql:467-485, identical in workers/schema_master.sql:53-71): the api_keys table has no `name`, `scopes`, or `updated_at` columns (confirmed columns: id, user_id, key_hash, key_prefix, label, tier, daily_limit, monthly_limit, active, created_at, last_used_at, expires_at, email, api_key, revoked, org_id, status). Confirmed no migration ever added them: grep for 'ALTER TABLE api_keys ADD COLUMN' across every workers/*.sql and workers/migrations/* file only shows org_id and status added (workers/schema_migration_prod_missing_columns_2026_07.sql:121,124); workers/schema_master.sql:4676-4680 contains the maintainer's own note confirming a later duplicate CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS block silently no-ops and production never gained name/scopes. This INSERT throws a 'no such column' SQL error on every single call, caught only by the handler's generic `catch (e) { return jsonResp({ error: e.message }, 500) }` (:986) — a 500 on every POST /api/developer/keys, with no functional workaround. listAPIKeys (:998, SELECT id,org_id,name,scopes,...) and revokeAPIKey/rotateAPIKey (:1006,1018, both `SET ... updated_at=?`) fail identically. CONFIRMED no auth: workers/src/index.js:7721-7723 dispatches `handleDeveloperPortal(request, env)` — no authCtx argument exists on this call at all, and createAPIKey's own signature (workers/src/handlers/developerPortal.js:971, `async function createAPIKey(request, env)`) takes none either — any caller, unauthenticated, can attempt to mint a key for any org_id they type in the request body (the attempt itself always 500s per above, but the missing gate is real and independent of the schema bug). CONFIRMED linked from the platform's own OpenAPI spec: API_CATALOG (workers/src/handlers/developerPortal.js:10, 'Developer Platform' group at :193-207) documents all four /keys endpoints; getOpenAPISpec() (:1251) mechanically generates the /api/developer/openapi spec route and its top-level alias at /api/openapi (workers/src/index.js:7726-7728) from this same catalog. CONFIRMED this is documented as onboarding step 1: workers/src/handlers/developerPortal.js:1102's quickstart response literally instructs 'Get your API key... POST /api/developer/keys' as the first step before any other portal feature can be used with real authentication.

notes: Highest-severity finding in this domain: a customer following this platform's own published, machine-readable quickstart guide has their very first API call fail with a 500, every time, with no auth even attempted first. This is a genuine 'time to first value' blocker for the API-first customer segment this portal exists to serve — distinct in kind from CAP-DEVPORTAL-002's silent-wrong-tier bug (that one succeeds with wrong data; this one never succeeds at all). Deliberately NOT fixed in this wave, for the same reason as CAP-DEVPORTAL-002 — see that entry's notes on the established pattern (document here, fix in a dedicated follow-up, per docs/capability-registry/PROGRAM_BOARD.md's own prior guidance not to bundle a security/correctness fix into a registry-population pass). The non-key portions of this handler (SDK generation, OpenAPI/Postman export, quickstart/auth-guide/changelog text, playground) are real, are called from frontend/automation-dashboard.html, and were NOT found broken this pass — but were also not exhaustively re-verified against live schema/behavior; routes_fully_enumerated is correctly false per this schema's own guidance for prefix/startsWith dispatchers (this handler alone documents 25+ endpoints in API_CATALOG's 'Developer Platform' group). A dedicated fix should (1) point createAPIKey/listAPIKeys/revokeAPIKey/rotateAPIKey at the real api_keys columns (or, better, delete this parallel implementation entirely and delegate to the canonical workers/src/auth/apiKeys.js's createApiKey() the way CAP-DEVPORTAL-001's other correct consumers already do — same recommendation applies to CAP-DEVPORTAL-002 and -004), and (2) add the missing auth gate consistent with the isRealUser(authCtx) pattern used at 30+ other routes in workers/src/index.js.

name: Growth/Plan API Key Provisioning
description: A fourth, independent API-key provisioning path tied to the marketing/growth funnel: given an email address and a pricing plan, mints an `sap_`-prefixed key intended to gate access by plan (starter/pro/enterprise) for programmatic growth/API-economy customers, separate from both the canonical self-service flow and the trial-key signup funnel.

frontend.evidence: No frontend page found calling /api/growth/api-key or /api/growth/api-usage (grep across frontend/*.html returns 0 matches) — reachable only via direct API call, e.g. from a growth/marketing automation tool outside this repo.

navigation.evidence: No frontend surface exists for this endpoint; not discoverable via any UI navigation path.

test_coverage.evidence: workers/test/apiKeyHashing.test.mjs:6 imports provisionApiKey/resolveApiKey directly, but the test's mock DB (:8-41) hand-simulates the INSERT with a plain JS array keyed on a regex match against the SQL string (`/INSERT INTO api_keys/.test(sql)`) rather than exercising a real schema — it does not, and structurally cannot, catch a column-mismatch or a missing-UNIQUE-constraint error. No other test file references handleProvisionApiKey or handleGetApiUsage.

verification.evidence: workers/src/services/apiRevenueEngine.js:312-339 and workers/src/handlers/growth.js:300-322 read directly. CONFIRMED bug — provisionApiKey (:319-327) issues `INSERT INTO api_keys (id, email, plan, api_key, active, created_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, 1, ?) ON CONFLICT(email) DO UPDATE SET plan=excluded.plan, api_key=excluded.api_key, active=1, updated_at=excluded.created_at`. Three independent failure modes, cross-checked against the same live schema used for CAP-DEVPORTAL-003 (workers/schema_bootstrap.sql:467-485): (1) `plan` and `updated_at` are not real columns (the table has `tier`, not `plan`, and no `updated_at` at all) — same 'no such column' class of failure as CAP-DEVPORTAL-003; (2) `key_hash` and `key_prefix` are declared `NOT NULL` with no DEFAULT, and this INSERT's column list omits both entirely — a NOT NULL constraint violation independent of the plan/updated_at issue; (3) `ON CONFLICT(email)` requires a UNIQUE constraint or index on exactly that column — the schema declares `email TEXT` with no UNIQUE marker anywhere in its definition or migration history, which SQLite rejects outright ('ON CONFLICT clause does not match any PRIMARY KEY or UNIQUE constraint'). Any one of these three is independently sufficient to make this INSERT fail on every call; caught only by the function's own generic `catch (err) { ...; return null; }` (:335-338), which handleProvisionApiKey (workers/src/handlers/growth.js:314) turns into a generic `key_provisioning_failed` 500. CONFIRMED no auth: handleProvisionApiKey (workers/src/handlers/growth.js:300-322) requires only an `email` field in the request body — no ownership/identity verification that the caller controls that email address — so, independent of the above schema failure, the design as written would let any caller attempt to provision or overwrite a plan/key for an arbitrary third party's email.

notes: Same bug class as CAP-DEVPORTAL-003 (unauthenticated + non-existent-column INSERT, always fails), found independently in a completely different file — the second confirmed occurrence in this domain, directly answering this wave's mandate to search for the same bug class across the domain and report every occurrence. Deliberately NOT fixed in this wave, same rationale as the two entries above. A dedicated fix should, at minimum, correct the column list to match the real api_keys schema (or add a genuine migration for a `plan` column plus a UNIQUE index on `email` if that's the intended design) and add an identity check before provisioning a key against an arbitrary email. Given three of four systems in this domain (CAP-DEVPORTAL-002, -003, -004) independently reimplement key issuance rather than calling the one correct, tested, schema-accurate function (workers/src/auth/apiKeys.js's createApiKey(), see CAP-DEVPORTAL-001), the most durable fix across all three is consolidation onto that one function rather than three separate column-list patches.
